definition: The sub-IG debt maturity wall (~$1.5-1.8T maturing 2025-27) creates a structural vulnerability where commodity shocks trigger a scissors dynamic: rising refinancing costs meet declining EBITDA. For a 6x levered borrower, 350bp all-in cost increase means $21M additional annual interest on $600M debt (42% increase), potentially rendering some borrowers unable to refinance at any spread.
evidence: 200bp tightening + 150bp spread widening = 350bp all-in cost increase
$21M additional annual interest on $600M debt for 6x levered borrower (42% increase on $50M base)
Scenario requires both commodity shock and monetary tightening simultaneously, which is the modal central bank response
open_questions: What share of the maturity wall consists of commodity-sensitive borrowers?
Can extend-and-pretend strategies defer the scissors dynamic, and if so, for how long?
